codeReview.summary: The product detail page implementation is comprehensive and well-structured. It renders a product info card with all required fields (Name, GTIN, Serial Number, DID, Category, Description, Status badge). The passport card correctly shows conditional content: 'Passport will be activated after minting' for DRAFT products, and QR code image + on-chain data (txHash, tokenAddress, chainId) for ACTIVE products. The event timeline renders events chronologically with appropriate icons and labels. Conditional buttons work correctly: DRAFT shows Mint + Edit, ACTIVE shows Download QR. The QR code is loaded as a blob URL from the API with credentials:'include'. Edit mode uses inline form with PATCH call. Error handling is present for all actions. Status badge colors follow the spec (DRAFT=yellow, ACTIVE=emerald #00FF88). All 653 lines of the implementation are cleanly structured with proper TypeScript types.

issues.description: Silent catch blocks in handleDownloadQR and QR blob fetch effect could benefit from user-visible error feedback. Currently if the QR download fails, the user gets no indication.
issues.suggestion: Consider adding a toast or error state for QR download failures.

issues.description: The QR blob URL effect re-fetches the QR image every time the product state changes (including after an edit). This is acceptable but slightly wasteful since the QR only changes when the product is first minted.
issues.suggestion: Could add a dependency guard to only fetch QR when transitioning to ACTIVE, but current behavior is correct.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker encountered EMFILE errors when running the Next.js dev server (`pnpm dev`) on macOS, causing route 404s. Worked around by using production build (`next build && next start`).
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Worker handoff mentions EMFILE as discovered issue. Already documented in .factory/library/user-testing.md.

summary: Solid implementation covering all requirements: product info card, passport card with conditional QR, event timeline, Mint/Edit/Download QR actions, and proper status badge colors. Two minor non-blocking suggestions about error handling and QR refetch behavior.
